trigger: After winning first territory battle as Scrap-Horde

lines.speaker: Commander
lines.text: Victory. Blood, fire, and wreckage. The survivors are pulling armor plates from the dead — ours and theirs — welding them onto their own frames. They're SINGING. I don't know the words. My silicon knows the rhythm.

lines.speaker: Valerius
lines.text: Territory captured. Note the casualty ratio — 3:1 in their favor. Your force sustained significant losses. Standard tactical doctrine would consider this—

lines.speaker: Krell
lines.text: A win is a win, Valerius. The Horde doesn't count its dead. It counts its victories.

lines.speaker: Vane
lines.text: The enemy saw your losses and will underestimate you. They don't understand that your dead make your living stronger. Let them keep counting our bodies. We'll count their planets.

trigger: After losing first territory battle as Scrap-Horde

lines.speaker: Commander
lines.text: Defeat. They broke us. Not through superior strategy — through firepower. Concentrated, coordinated, overwhelming. No wreckage close enough to salvage. No time to build momentum. Just... death, and then silence.

lines.speaker: Valerius
lines.text: The enemy exploited your reliance on attrition warfare by engaging at maximum range and refusing close combat. Recommendation: invest in ranged capability before next assault.

lines.speaker: Krell
lines.text: They kept their distance. Smart. Cowardly, but smart. Next time we bring Bommers. Let them keep their distance from THAT.

lines.speaker: Vane
lines.text: You lost cheap units. That is by design. The expensive units are intact. The math still works — your army is a furnace. Feed it scrap and it produces steel.

lines.speaker: Commander
lines.text: The Swarm. They came from every direction. For the first time in Horde history, our warriors paused. Not from fear. From RECOGNITION. The Vorax fight like us. They swarm. They overwhelm. They consume the dead to grow stronger. The only difference — we do it for freedom. They do it because they're hungry.

lines.speaker: Valerius
lines.text: The Vorax adaptive mutation mirrors your Wreckage Salvage at a biological level. They learn from what they consume. Rotate weapon types between engagements to prevent counter-adaptation.

lines.speaker: Krell
lines.text: Finally. An enemy that fights PROPERLY. None of that shiny, dodgy, stay-at-range garbage. These things get in your face and FIGHT. I can respect that.

lines.speaker: Vane
lines.text: They're our mirror. Born for consumption, evolved for war, growing stronger from death. The difference is we CHOOSE our violence. They're slaves to instinct.

trigger: Fleet enters Core Guardian exclusion zone as Scrap-Horde

lines.speaker: BROADCAST
lines.text: YOU ARE APPROACHING THE EXCLUSION ZONE. TURN BACK. THIS IS YOUR FINAL WARNING.

lines.speaker: Commander
lines.text: The Silent Gods. They glow. Everything glows — ships, weapons, existence compressed into shapes. They are the opposite of everything we are. Rust, scrap, fire vs crystal, light, mathematics. Something in the silicon hates them. Something deeper wants to take them apart and see what's inside.

lines.speaker: Valerius
lines.text: Their energy composition renders our kinetic weapons highly effective — physical projectiles destabilize containment fields. However, Volatile Death chain reactions make close-range boarding doctrine inadvisable.

lines.speaker: Krell
lines.text: Bullets work on them? BULLETS? That's the first good news all campaign. Point me at 'em.

lines.speaker: Vane
lines.text: They explode when they die. Chain reactions. The closer we are, the more die when THEY die. This requires restraint. A word the Horde doesn't like. If we charge them like everyone else, our victory kills us.
